headline: Philip Pullman’s The Rose Field — the Book of Dust series ends with a quest for the source of imagination
summary: The final part of a second trilogy by the author of ‘His Dark Materials’ follows an adult Lyra in her alternate world of daemons and angels
body_text:
Thirty years ago, the world was introduced to Lyra Silvertongue with the publication of Philip Pullman’s novel Northern Lights in 1995. In the US the book was published as The Golden Compass, a title referring to the “alethiometer”, an instrument for divining knowledge with which 11-year-old Lyra develops a mystic proficiency. It was followed by The Subtle Knife (1997) and The Amber Spyglass (2000): between them, they constituted a trilogy known as His Dark Materials and sold more than 22mn books worldwide.

Back then, Lyra was fearless, curious and a teller of tall tales so dazzling that they could get her out of any tight spot. In His Dark Materials, Lyra and her friend Will carve doorways into other worlds, redeem humanity from a purgatorial afterlife and end up destroying God.

Pullman, then sometimes associated with the New Atheist movement led by Richard Dawkins and Christopher Hitchens, invited a generation of adolescent readers to question the constraints of religion and embrac
